goal: Land `crates/ucil-treesitter/src/tag_cache.rs` with a `TagCache` type backed by LMDB (via the `heed` crate) that maps `(file_path, mtime) -> Vec<ExtractedSymbol>` with warm reads completing in <1ms on a fixture input. Closes feature P1-W2-F04 and unblocks downstream reuse of the extractor results by the daemon file-watcher path (a future WO wires it in).
plan_summary: Add a new sibling module `tag_cache.rs` to `crates/ucil-treesitter/src/` alongside the frozen `parser.rs` (WO-0005) and `symbols.rs` (WO-0017). The module exposes: (1) a `TagCacheError` enum via `thiserror` — variants for LMDB IO, serialization, and key-encoding failures — marked `#[non_exhaustive]`; (2) a `TagCache` struct holding a `heed::Env` + a typed `heed::Database<Bytes, Bytes>` — one Env per `TagCache` instance, opened via `TagCache::open(dir: &Path)`; the daemon-level `OnceLock<heed::Env>` sharing is deliberately out-of-scope (P1-W3-F01 territory); (3) core CRUD — `get(&self, path: &Path, mtime: SystemTime) -> Result<Option<Vec<ExtractedSymbol>>, TagCacheError>`, `put(&self, path: &Path, mtime: SystemTime, symbols: &[ExtractedSymbol]) -> Result<(), TagCacheError>`, `invalidate_path(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<(), TagCacheError>` (removes ALL entries for a given path regardless of mtime — iterates the path-prefix range), `len(&self) -> Result<usize>`, `clear(&self) -> Result<()>` — each routes through a `heed::RoTxn` or `heed::RwTxn` correctly. Key encoding: `[path_bytes].[0x00].[mtime_nanos_i128_be]` — the path's `OsStr::as_encoded_bytes()` on Unix, concatenated with a single NUL sentinel (impossible inside a filesystem path on any mainstream OS) and the mtime's nanoseconds-since-epoch encoded big-endian as `i128` so LMDB's default byte-ordering sorts entries lexicographically by `(path, mtime)` — the BE encoding makes `invalidate_path` cheap via a range scan bounded by `[path_bytes, 0x00]` .. `[path_bytes, 0x01]`. Value encoding: `bincode` v1 or v2 (executor picks; `serde_json` is acceptable if cycle-time is favourable, but `bincode` is the expected choice for L1 cache perf). The `ExtractedSymbol` + `SymbolKind` types already derive `Serialize` / `Deserialize` (WO-0017). Module-root flat tests per DEC-0005 — NO `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { }` wrapper anywhere in `tag_cache.rs` — so the frozen nextest selector `tag_cache::` resolves every test as `ucil_treesitter::tag_cache::<test_name>`. Workspace `Cargo.toml` grows two entries under `[workspace.dependencies]`: `heed` and `bincode`; the consumer crate `crates/ucil-treesitter/Cargo.toml` pulls them via `workspace = true`. If LMDB system-link problems surface on CI, `heed`'s default static-link via `lmdb-master3-sys` is sufficient — no ADR needed unless a genuine build failure appears.
scope_in: crates/ucil-treesitter/src/tag_cache.rs (new module, ~400-550 lines incl. tests + rustdoc)

* `TagCacheError` enum via `thiserror`, `#[non_exhaustive]`, variants: `Io(std::io::Error)`, `Lmdb(heed::Error)`, `Serialize(bincode::Error)` (or `serde_json::Error` if executor picks JSON), `InvalidPath(PathBuf)` — plus `#[from]` on each underlying error
* `TagCache` struct holding `env: heed::Env` + `db: heed::Database<heed::types::Bytes, heed::types::Bytes>` — fields `pub(crate)` at most; the struct itself is `pub`

* `TagCache::open(dir: &Path) -> Result<Self, TagCacheError>` — creates `dir` if missing, opens an LMDB env with sensible defaults (map size 1 GiB; max_dbs = 1; max_readers = 126 default), opens/creates the unnamed default database with `(Bytes, Bytes)` codecs

* `TagCache::get(&self, path: &Path, mtime: SystemTime) -> Result<Option<Vec<ExtractedSymbol>>, TagCacheError>` — read-only txn; key from `encode_key(path, mtime)`; decodes value via `bincode::deserialize` (or equivalent)

* `TagCache::put(&self, path: &Path, mtime: SystemTime, symbols: &[ExtractedSymbol]) -> Result<(), TagCacheError>` — read-write txn; key as above; value is `bincode::serialize(symbols)?`; commits the txn

* `TagCache::invalidate_path(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<usize, TagCacheError>` — iterates the range `[path_bytes || 0x00]` .. `[path_bytes || 0x01]` and deletes each; returns number of entries removed
* `TagCache::len(&self) -> Result<usize, TagCacheError>` — returns the total number of entries (via `db.len(&txn)?`)
* `TagCache::clear(&self) -> Result<(), TagCacheError>` — truncates the database

* `encode_key(path: &Path, mtime: SystemTime) -> Result<Vec<u8>, TagCacheError>` — private helper; returns `[path_as_encoded_bytes, 0x00, mtime_nanos_i128_be]`; errors if mtime is before UNIX_EPOCH and `duration_since` fails
* `decode_value(bytes: &[u8]) -> Result<Vec<ExtractedSymbol>, TagCacheError>` — private helper wrapping `bincode::deserialize`

* `#[tracing::instrument(level = "debug", skip(self))]` on `get`, `put`, `invalidate_path` — span names `ucil.treesitter.tag_cache_get`, `ucil.treesitter.tag_cache_put`, `ucil.treesitter.tag_cache_invalidate` per master-plan §15.2
crates/ucil-treesitter/src/lib.rs — add `pub mod tag_cache;` + `pub use tag_cache::{TagCache, TagCacheError};`

crates/ucil-treesitter/Cargo.toml — `heed = { workspace = true }` and `bincode = { workspace = true }` under `[dependencies]`; executor confirms `lmdb` static-linking works on the local build (master-plan phase-log lists this as a known risk)
Cargo.toml (workspace root) — add `heed = "0.20"` and `bincode = "1"` to `[workspace.dependencies]` (executor may pick a different stable version line; document the choice in the commit body)
Module-root tests in `tag_cache.rs` (flat — NOT under `mod tests { }`, per DEC-0005):

* `tag_cache_put_then_get_returns_symbols` — opens a cache in a `tempfile::TempDir`, writes a 2-symbol `Vec<ExtractedSymbol>` for `(some_path, some_mtime)`, reads back, asserts the full `Vec` round-trips (names, kinds, line ranges, signatures, doc_comments)
* `tag_cache_get_missing_returns_none` — on a freshly-opened cache, `get(nonexistent_path, now)` returns `Ok(None)`
* `tag_cache_different_mtime_is_distinct_entry` — writes once at `mtime_a`, writes a different `Vec` at `mtime_b` for the same path, asserts both can be read back and are distinct
* `tag_cache_invalidate_path_removes_all_mtimes` — puts 3 entries for the same path at 3 different mtimes, calls `invalidate_path`, asserts `.len() == 0` AND all 3 `get` calls return `None`
* `tag_cache_invalidate_path_leaves_other_paths_intact` — puts entries for path_a + path_b, calls `invalidate_path(path_a)`, asserts path_b's entries are still retrievable
* `tag_cache_reopen_persists_entries` — opens cache, writes an entry, drops the `TagCache`, re-opens the same dir, asserts the entry is readable

* `tag_cache_roundtrip_preserves_all_symbol_fields` — stores an `ExtractedSymbol` with every field populated (including `signature: Some(...)`, `doc_comment: Some(...)`) and asserts bitwise equality on read

* `tag_cache_empty_vec_roundtrips` — stores an empty `Vec<ExtractedSymbol>`, reads back `Some(vec![])` (NOT `None`) — ensures the distinction between 'no entry' and 'entry with empty symbol list' is preserved

* `tag_cache_warm_read_under_1ms` — populates a fixture with 100 entries; performs 1000 warm reads; asserts the **median** latency is <1 ms (use `std::time::Instant` + sort-and-index, NOT mean, to avoid GC-like outliers skewing; a debug-build slop factor of ≤ 3× is acceptable — target is 1ms median on `cargo test --release`, 3ms on debug, the test adapts via `cfg!(debug_assertions)`)
* `tag_cache_len_reflects_writes` — starts empty, `len() == 0`; after 5 writes, `len() == 5`; after `clear()`, `len() == 0`

* `tag_cache_key_ordering_is_lexicographic` — writes entries for the same path at mtimes `t1 < t2 < t3`; iterates the db and asserts the retrieved order matches `t1, t2, t3` (not a guarantee we depend on for correctness, but a property test that pins key-encoding to avoid accidental regressions)

Rustdoc on every public item: `TagCache`, `TagCacheError`, `TagCache::open`, `TagCache::get`, `TagCache::put`, `TagCache::invalidate_path`, `TagCache::len`, `TagCache::clear` — module-level rustdoc cites master-plan §18 Phase 1 Week 2 line 1735 and §2.1 Layer 1 (tiered cache), documents the key encoding, and documents the <1ms warm-read perf target
`#![allow(clippy::module_name_repetitions)]` in `tag_cache.rs` IF pedantic flags `TagCache`/`TagCacheError` inside the `tag_cache` module (same precedent as `parser.rs` and `symbols.rs`)
scope_out: Wiring the cache into the file-watcher path — belongs to `P1-W4-F04` (tree-sitter → knowledge-graph pipeline) or a later daemon WO
L0 in-memory LRU cache layer — belongs to Phase 4 Week 20 (master-plan §18 line 1906 'Full tiered cache')
Cross-branch sharing — the cache is per-branch; `.ucil/branches/<branch>/tags.lmdb` is the on-disk target but the dir convention is set by the daemon, not this crate
Automatic TTL / eviction policies — out of scope; cache grows until `invalidate_path` or `clear` is called explicitly
Any modification to `crates/ucil-treesitter/src/parser.rs` — frozen by WO-0005
Any modification to `crates/ucil-treesitter/src/symbols.rs` — frozen by WO-0017
Any modification to `crates/ucil-core/**` — `ExtractedSymbol` is serde-ready; no bridge to `ucil_core::types::Symbol` is introduced here (that is P1-W4-F04 territory)
Adding a `ucil-daemon` dependency edge to `ucil-treesitter` — phase-log invariant 5 forbids it
Population from tree-sitter parse — the cache is a passive store; the populator/invalidator is the daemon's file-watcher (future WO)

New ADR — `heed`/LMDB is the master-plan's named choice (§18 Week 2 line 1735, §9.1 data layout `tags.lmdb`); raise a proposed ADR ONLY if a real ambiguity or build failure surfaces (e.g. `lmdb-master3-sys` refuses to compile on this machine)
acceptance: New `tag_cache` module exists at `crates/ucil-treesitter/src/tag_cache.rs` and is declared + re-exported from `lib.rs`
`cargo nextest run -p ucil-treesitter tag_cache::` matches ≥ 10 tests, all passing (the P1-W2-F04 acceptance selector)
`cargo clippy -p ucil-treesitter --all-targets -- -D warnings` is clean (pedantic + nursery, inherits from lib.rs)
`cargo doc -p ucil-treesitter --no-deps` is clean (no broken intra-doc links)
Reality-check oracle: stashing `tag_cache.rs` causes the selector `tag_cache::` to return 0 matching tests (or cargo build fails); popping restores — `scripts/reality-check.sh P1-W2-F04`
No mocks of `heed`, `lmdb`, or `bincode` — tests use a real LMDB env backed by `tempfile::TempDir`
`Parser` public surface from WO-0005 remains byte-for-byte unchanged (`git diff` proves `parser.rs` = 0 lines changed)
`SymbolExtractor` public surface from WO-0017 remains byte-for-byte unchanged (`git diff` proves `symbols.rs` = 0 lines changed)
Module-root tests are flat — no `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { }` wrapper anywhere in `tag_cache.rs` — per DEC-0005
Warm-read perf target: the `tag_cache_warm_read_under_1ms` test passes (median latency <1ms release / <3ms debug on ≥100 iterations)
acceptance_criteria: cargo nextest run -p ucil-treesitter 'tag_cache::' --status-level=pass --hide-progress-bar 2>&1 | grep -E '^\s+PASS' | awk 'END{exit (NR < 10)}'
context_refs: master-plan:§18 Phase 1 Week 2 line 1735 ('Implement tag cache in LMDB: file_path + mtime → [Symbol]')
master-plan:§2.1 Layer 1 (Tiered cache: L0 in-memory LRU, L1 local index/LMDB <10ms, L2 full tool invocation)
master-plan:§15.2 Tracing span naming convention (ucil.<layer>.<op>) — span names ucil.treesitter.tag_cache_{get,put,invalidate}
rules:.claude/rules/rust-style.md (thiserror; pedantic+nursery; tracing; no unwrap outside #[cfg(test)]; no unsafe without ADR; one crate per Cargo.toml; edition 2021)

phase-log:ucil-build/phase-log/01-phase-1/CLAUDE.md (invariant 1: no mocks of LMDB; invariant 4: OnceLock<heed::Env> is daemon-layer, NOT this WO; invariant 5: ucil-treesitter depends only on ucil-core + tree-sitter crates; Risks §: heed/LMDB on CI may need lmdb-sys bundled feature — document via ADR if it breaks)
lessons_applied: Module-root flat tests (no `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { }` wrapper) per DEC-0005 — the frozen selector `tag_cache::` is a path prefix; placing tests flat mirrors `symbols.rs` (WO-0017) and `parser.rs` (WO-0005) and avoids the WO-0006 / WO-0007 selector-mismatch failure mode

No mocks of LMDB (phase-log invariant 1) — tests open a real `heed::Env` under a `tempfile::TempDir` and read/write through the real `Database<Bytes, Bytes>`; `tempfile` is already a dev-dependency in the workspace
thiserror + `#[non_exhaustive]` on `TagCacheError`; use `#[from]` to auto-convert `heed::Error`, `bincode::Error`, and `std::io::Error`
Do NOT modify `parser.rs` — WO-0005 frozen; `git diff == 0` acceptance check enforces byte-for-byte preservation
Do NOT modify `symbols.rs` — WO-0017 frozen; `git diff == 0` acceptance check enforces byte-for-byte preservation
Do NOT modify `crates/ucil-core/**` — the bridge from `ExtractedSymbol` → `ucil_core::types::Symbol` is P1-W4-F04 territory
Do NOT add a `ucil-daemon` dependency edge — phase-log invariant 5; `! grep -q 'ucil-daemon' Cargo.toml` acceptance check enforces

If `heed` / `lmdb-master3-sys` fails to build on this machine (libclang / bundled-feature issue noted in phase-log Risks), STOP and raise a proposed ADR rather than grinding on env-config fixes — do NOT silently switch to `sled` or drop LMDB without a written decision record
Conventional Commits with `Phase: 1`, `Feature: P1-W2-F04`, `Work-order: WO-0018` trailers on every commit; push after every commit; no `--amend` after push; no force-push

If the module file exceeds ~400 lines (likely — 11 tests + rustdoc + per-encoding helpers), the DEC-0001 / DEC-0005 module-introduction-commit precedent permits a single coherent `feat(treesitter): add TagCache LMDB store` commit up to ~550 lines when splitting would produce dead-code intermediate states (e.g. a `TagCache` without `get` or a `get` without a matching `put`)

Session-abandonment prevention (cf. RCA for WO-0012): before ending the session, verify that (a) at least one `.rs` diff against `origin/main` exists on the feature branch, (b) every commit has been pushed, AND (c) `0018-ready-for-review.md` has been written — if any is missing, either keep working or write an escalation explaining the blocker

Warm-read perf target is a real acceptance gate (<1ms median on release builds) — do not disable the perf test to satisfy acceptance; if it genuinely cannot be met, raise a proposed ADR with the measured numbers and a proposed revised target
